CommonWords.EndOfEarlyAccessBody:
Congratulations! You have reached the current end of the main story.

This game is still in development, and our goal is to continue the story in future updates.

Until then, there's a lot more to see and do! You can use your Water Walk ability to reach new areas, with more resources to discover and new crafting recipes and upgrades to unlock.

Please let us know what you think of the game, we love to hear from our players.

CommonWords.DeliveryMenuIntroMessageBody:
Good job setting up the Resource Delivery Cannon, Exofarmer! Here you’ll be able to load and send supplies to all the nearby colonies in need of assistance. It’ll no doubt be hard work, but with every delivery completed, you’ll be able to increase the <Mark1>Power Limit</> of your mech, making larger deliveries manageable. As part of the project, we’ll also be able to transfer <Mark1>various rewards</> to you for reaching delivery Milestones.

Once all Milestones have been reached, you will receive official ownership of <Mark1>{PlanetName}</>.

Good luck, Exofarmer!

DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding05_TextEntry:
<Mark1>Tall</> society was unequivocally divided into three separate castes based on occupation. While their names are not fully translatable, the Exoarchaeological Society has decided to label them with approximate Latin titles.

The vast majority of the population were the <Mark1>Labori</>, collective-minded and with little to no personal effects, proud of their skilled hands and tasked with constructing whatever was needed.

From this group, scientifically gifted individuals may rise to join the <Mark1>Cogitus</>, using their brilliant minds to develop the technology required to fulfill the visionaries' plans.

An even smaller portion of the <Mark1>Labori</> would exhibit individualistic traits, and those who would go so far as to give themselves a name would be recognised as <Mark1>Artifex</>, the third and most exclusive caste. They were the visionaries, wielding the power to direct society's path forward.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding09_TextEntry:
After receiving samples from several different planets across the Frontier (our gratitude to contributors like yourself cannot be overstated) we have made a groundbreaking discovery!

We are seeing clear similarities between the <Mark1>Tall</>'s genetically modified fauna and the land they walk on.

It appears that by the end of their reign, the <Mark1>Tall</> were capable of creating entire planets according to their designs!

Soil samples indicate that a majority of the Lightyear Frontier was created this way, explaining the overabundance of habitable worlds.

We have reported our findings to the Prometheus Astronomical Fellowship, who will have the arduous task of fully reconstructing our understanding of this corner of the galaxy.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding06_TextEntry:
All members of the <Mark1>Tall</> wore masks at all times. These masks were adorned with a crest symbolizing their caste, and the <Mark1>Artifex</> masks were uniquely decorated to distinguish each individual.

Members of the <Mark1>Artifex</> were characterized by lithe and delicate builds, wearing ornate dresses embroidered with their name, always proud of what set them apart from others.

The <Mark1>Cogitus</> represented the middle-ground, generally clad in plain robes with colorful fringes reflecting their proximity to the elite caste.

The <Mark1>Labori</> had fewer clothes in general, practical garb with nothing that signified who they were beyond their occupations. They were characterized by their bulky builds from their constant manual labor.

Some <Mark1>Labori</> would feel affinity for certain members of the <Mark1>Artifex</>, adorning their mask with copies of their crest. They did not possess the individuality to rise as <Mark1>Artifex</> themselves but found comfort and community in identifying as a follower, swearing themselves to their cause.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding03_TextEntry:
As quickly as they rose and spread their population across the Frontier, their downfall was far more rapid. Something caused their civilization to end almost overnight, but that cause has yet to be found.

Radiometric dating of their ruins shows that none were constructed after a certain date some 3000 years ago. While there are some scattered signs of famine and bloodshed, most dwellings appear to have been simply abandoned, with no clear cause for their departure.

Where they went is anyone's guess. The search for answers continues.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding16_TextEntry:
Considering the vast number of surface ruins found that correlate to living spaces, it is clear that the few <Mark1>Vault</>s discovered across the Frontier could not feasibly house everyone.

As the <Mark1>Vault</>s reached completion, this fact must have become apparent to the <Mark1>Tall</> themselves. Even among the collective-minded <Mark1>Labori</>, few would have been content to stand aside and accept certain doom while the privileged few hid underground.

What began as a unified effort to save their species quickly dissolved into a desperate struggle for individual survival. Worksites were abandoned, supply caravans raided, and infighting began.

It is possible more <Mark1>Vault</>s had been planned, but we have found no trace of them.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding18_TextEntry:
Despite the destruction of their leadership and their society in tatters, the <Mark1>Labori</> still had a number of <Mark1>Vault</>s left available.

Through violence or negotiations, some made their way inside, while the rest were left aboveground to accept their fate. But those who took shelter in the <Mark1>Vault</>s would not fare any better.

As the years passed, the <Mark1>Miasma</> showed no signs of receding, and eventually the <Mark1>Vault</>s would run out of food. One after the other, the last of the <Mark1>Tall</> starved in the deep dark, waiting for a day that would not come.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding12_TextEntry:
Many of the entries detail daily life operating the machine, making adjustments according to <Mark1>Artifex</> whims and requesting new parts for the <Mark1>Labori</> to construct and install.

However, it all came to a stop when the <Mark1>Genesis Device</> exploded. The planet it was in the process of creating suddenly came apart and created a massive outburst of energy that destroyed the entire facility. Pieces of half-formed planet were flung across the entire Frontier.

The cause was unknown. Was there a flaw in the work of the <Mark1>Labori</>s, a miscalculation by the <Mark1>Cogitus</>, or overambition of the <Mark1>Artifex</>, pushing the machine too far?
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding04_TextEntry:
Much can be gleaned from mundane objects, even a patch of fabric will become a precious artifact if it spends a few generations hidden in the dirt.

From recent research contributions in the form of clothes, tools, cutlery, and similar, we've been able to get an understanding of the <Mark1>Tall</>'s social structures and values.

With a strong focus on creativity and imagination, their society was built around sole individuals that garnered support and idolization with their mastery of creative expression. Rising from obscurity with unique artistic ideas and thus gaining control was their way of life.

This led to a social hierarchy where these individuals were put far above everyone else, who had the collective responsibility to fulfill their creative vision.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding08_TextEntry:
Much of the research that has been conducted on the fauna inhabiting the Lightyear Frontier is conclusive in the fact that some form of gene manipulation has occurred.

The timeframe for the unnatural deviations can be traced back to the same period as the ruins scattered throughout the Frontier. Furthermore, ruins containing probable bio-engineering equipment have been reported by homesteaders on fringe planets.

This has formed the unanimous conclusion among the Exoarchaeological Society that the <Mark1>Tall</>'s creative ambitions outgrew architecture and that the creatures of the land should also adhere to their vision.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding01_TextEntry:
Not much is known about the ancient civilization that once called the Lightyear Frontier its home. Despite the numerous ruins spread across multiple planets, the governing board of <Mark1>Prometheus Station</> remains reluctant to spend resources on archaeological expeditions to learn more.

Thankfully, progress has begun to pick up thanks to research contributions from recent arrivals like yourself, and we're learning new things every day.

So far, we have unearthed several key pieces of information about this lost civilization.
For one, we know they called themselves the <Mark1>Tall</>.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding10_TextEntry:
Our recent breakthrough has changed everything. The Exoarchaeological Society has been elevated from hobbyist activity to one of the foremost research fields on the whole station!

As a result of this massive influx of resources, we have finally come across our holy grail, a trove of <Mark1>Tall</> journal log entries.

If our carbon-14 measurements are accurate, these entries were written shortly before or simultaneous to the <Mark1>Tall</>'s downfall.

Translations are still underway, but are expected to reveal long awaited answers for how their final moments played out.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding15_TextEntry:
Realising they could not outrun the <Mark1>Miasma</>, the <Mark1>Tall</> elected to outlast it.

In a council that excluded the <Mark1>Cogitus</>, it was decided that they would build underground <Mark1>Vault</>s, shelters filled with food and water. It would be enough to sustain their inhabitants for a generation, surely long enough for the <Mark1>Miasma</> to subside.

The <Mark1>Artifex</> and <Mark1>Labori</> put all their efforts towards this endeavor, but the <Mark1>Cogitus</> were prevented from helping. Having caused the disaster, they could not be trusted to avert it.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding17_Title: The End of the Tall I
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding18_Title: The End of the Tall II
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding09_Title: The Making of the Lightyear Frontier
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding13_TextEntry:
The device's destruction left no trace of the cause, but a tribunal was still held to determine who was responsible for the disaster.

The <Mark1>Artifex</> were quickly absolved of wrongdoing, if their designs had been too strenuous on the machine, they should have been warned ahead of time.

Similarly, the <Mark1>Labori</>s were found innocent. They only did as they were told and were not expected to question the instructions they were given. Additionally, they had by far the greatest death toll in the explosion and had no reason to self-sabotage.

In the end, all blame was placed firmly on the <Mark1>Cogitus</>. The court could not agree if the error was born from negligence or a willful defiance against the <Mark1>Artifex</>. Nevertheless, from that day on, they were shunned.

This marks the end of this set of documents, their author's fate is regrettably left unknown. However, we have many other texts awaiting translation and our understanding expands day by day.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding17_TextEntry:
The final blow to seal the fate of the <Mark1>Tall</> may have been the sabotage of the <Mark1>Artifex</> <Mark1>Vault</>.

What remains of its layout clearly shows ample space for the entire <Mark1>Artifex</> caste and their sworn followers. However, for reasons unknown, the <Mark1>Artifex</> changed their minds and barred their <Mark1>Labori</> adherents from their promised salvation.

In an instant, admiration turned to animosity, and the <Mark1>Labori</> used their skill to destabilize the <Mark1>Vault</>'s inner workings before leaving. Soon after the <Mark1>Artifex</> took their rightful place and locked the door behind them, the entire facility went up in flames.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding07_TextEntry:
The general theme of their architectural projects was seemingly based in vanity rather than practicality. Many ruin sites discovered throughout the Frontier are quite massive and visually impressive, but their general application, if any, is difficult to discern.

Nevertheless, the construction and maintenance of these structures must have required access to immense levels of energy. The leading theory is that they somehow harnessed latent energies contained within the various crystals found across the Frontier.

Many ruin sites found near crystal-rich mountain ranges corroborate this theory, but their method of energy extraction remains a mystery.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding11_TextEntry:
The translation work has yielded fruit and it appears to be a series of memos written by one of the <Mark1>Cogitus</>, reflecting on their work to create planets.

Creating planets required a machine of massive scale, bigger than the Earth's own moon. This <Mark1>Genesis Device</> was the culmination of the <Mark1>Tall</>'s prowess in all fields: The <Mark1>Artifex</> vision, the <Mark1>Cogitus</> ingenuity, and the <Mark1>Labori</>s craftsmanship.

It was capable of creating worlds out of nothing but space dust and dark matter, making way for endless attempts at paradise by the <Mark1>Artifex</>, each iteration closer to perfection than the last.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding21_TextEntry:
This concludes all we know of the demise of this once great civilization, but history did not end with them.

The <Mark1>Miasma</>'s main source of energy was the <Mark1>Tall</>'s crystal power generators, and as they began to break down from centuries of disrepair, the <Mark1>Miasma</> began to recede.

As the <Mark1>Miasma</> dissipated, life began its return to the Frontier. While the largest and most impressive of the <Mark1>Tall</>'s artificial lifeforms were fully wiped out, many of their smaller creations had endured in burrows and caves deep beneath the surface.

Emerging to a completely new world, the artificial creatures began diverging from their genetic dispositions. Over generations, they would mutate into the forms we recognise today, their docile and friendly nature the only hint of their bio-engineered origins.

By the time humanity arrived in the Frontier, the planets had recovered to mostly healthy and hospitable ecosystems. Still, some of the <Mark1>Miasma</>'s taint may remain.

A recent theory supposes that many of the toxic weeds and slimes found across the Frontier are a dormant form of the <Mark1>Miasma</>, now far less prolific due to lacking its main energy source.

Whether or not that is the case, on behalf of the Exoarchaeological Society, I want to thank you for your contributions to our research.

Together we may better understand our place in the universe.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding19_TextEntry:
We have uncovered a new set of journal entries, written by one of the <Mark1>Cogitus</>. Their account offers a completely different perspective on the last days of the <Mark1>Tall</>.

This entire time, the <Mark1>Cogitus</> had warned the other castes of their miscalculation, the <Mark1>Miasma</> would last for at least a millenium.

But their disgraced status meant their words fell on deaf ears, and they were left to fend for themselves as the <Mark1>Artifex</> and <Mark1>Labori</> set their plan into motion.

The <Mark1>Cogitus</> knew there was no certain path to their species' survival, but eventually they decided on the solution with the best odds.

With the help of the discontented <Mark1>Labori</> left aboveground, they constructed a tomb of Cryo-Pods. Far more efficient than the <Mark1>Vault</>s, anyone who wanted a pod could have one.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding14_TextEntry:
We have yet to find another first hand account from the same period, but we have various fragments and passages that together paint a picture of the aftermath.

The consequences of the <Mark1>Genesis Device</>'s destruction were not immediately apparent, but over time a <Mark1>Miasma</> began spreading from where it had once stood.

It was comprised of the remnants of its final malformed planet. It fed off the crystalline energies of <Mark1>Tall</> technology, spreading like a wildfire and superceding all plant life it came across.

Its toxic spores were harmful to breathe, so once the <Mark1>Miasma</> made landfall on a planet, it would rapidly lose its ability to sustain organic life.

The <Mark1>Tall</> began to flee from planet to planet, but the <Mark1>Miasma</> continued to spread as chunks of the misbegotten planet fell across the Frontier.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding02_TextEntry:
While no fully intact <Mark1>Tall</> bodies have been discovered so far, it is clear that the name did not refer to their stature.

Remains of their civilization have been found on every habitable planet in the Frontier, and the buildings' proportions are close to human height.

Instead, the <Mark1>Tall</> seems to refer to a proud mindset, standing above all others. It is clear from their technological prowess and multi-planetary conquest that they were the dominant species of the Milky Way.

For a while.
DT_ArtifactLoreEntries.Finding20_TextEntry:
While they had secured the resources and manpower to complete their Cryo-Pod plan, the <Mark1>Cogitus</> themselves knew of several flaws.

Even at the peak of their cryo-tech advancements, they could not build pods that would reliably last well over a thousand years. The vast majority of those who went to sleep in those pods would not wake up again.

Even accepting that risk, the success of the plan was left to randomness: Since they could not accurately calculate the end of the <Mark1>Miasma</>'s reign, they could not set a time for the cryo-pods to thaw.

Instead, they put their hopes in the improbable odds that once the <Mark1>Miasma</> had cleared, new intelligent life would one day find them and wake them from their slumber.

The entries fail to tell us where these Cryo-pods were built, and so far we have not found any clues to their location.

DT_Findings.AlienHarbour_LoreEntry:
From what I can tell, this was a loading dock for seafaring cargo vessels. I'm guessing they were utilizing the same water traversal tech you installed on your mech, must be far more energy efficient compared to aerial transportation. 

I've scanned the containers and they seem to contain traces of food supplies, long since turned to dust. There are so many of them strewn about, it seems like a full shipment never made it wherever it was supposed to go.
DT_Findings.PowerStation_LoreEntry:
It's clear this facility was created to extract energy from the crystals we've seen littered around the planet, though I can't fathom the method. What's strange to me is that even in this broken state, this station is capable of a much higher power output than what it's currently going for. It's like someone put it on power-saving mode and then left.

Even so, it's a sizable amount of energy and it's clearly used for more than keeping the lights on. The transfer cables run into the ocean and then the signal is lost, I've got no clue where they go.
DT_Findings.AlienLandingPad_LoreEntry:
Scanning the local soil layers indicates numerous instances of short-term exposure to extreme amounts of heat. Furthermore, judging by the equipment you unearthed and the clearly intentional flattening of the area, I can only conclude this was a landing pad for spacecraft. However, none of your findings indicate the handling of cargo, so I hypothesize these ships were only used to transport individuals.

Whether that's true or not, this strongly indicates whoever came before us inhabited more than just this planet.
DT_Findings.HallOfFame_LoreEntry: The clearly ornamental remains within these ruins indicate that this was a place of recognition for highly regarded individuals or objects. However, surface analysis of the samples you provided reveals that the deterioration was not only caused by time but by blunt force as well. Perhaps someone wasn’t entirely satisfied with whatever was honored here?
DT_Findings.Village_LoreEntry:
The remains in this area strongly suggest that this could have been some form of settlement. The relics you've found indicate many residents per home, which strikes me as an oddity since there is no lack of space on the planet, then or now.

Your samples also contain particulate traces of minerals from the nearby mountain, so I theorize the main purpose of the settlement was gathering and refining metals. However, your search did not uncover any remains of the refined products. What were they making and where was it taken?
DT_Findings.AlienVault_LoreEntry: This is a vault that needs some more lore? [WIP]
DT_Findings.BioLab_LoreEntry:
While the site has been almost torn apart entirely, it's clear from the remaining equipment that this was a scientific facility. Based on the samples you've found, this place was used to biogenetically engineer the flora and fauna inhabiting this planet. The process might have created the invasive waste product we've seen infesting the area.

However, the fact that so many samples are left intact indicates the lab was abandoned in a hurry, with no time spent to secure the facility. With that in mind, perhaps the giant tree engulfing this facility was an experiment left unsupervised?
DT_Findings.AlienDam_LoreEntry:
Your samples revealed more than I expected. It looks like this thing was built in two rounds. The first part was the base, which is coated in some kind of hydrophobic materal, making me think this was originally a water dam, not a wall.

The second part was built on top of the dam at a much later date. My guess is that by that point the sea level on the planet had changed, and it was no longer needed for its original purpose. So they added the elaborate architecture over the blocky original and installed the gates at the bottom. What for? The answers are probably on the other side.

DT_InspectionPoints.IPMonumentFountain_Content: A surprisingly modest construction, compared to the other ruins you've seen on the planet. Maybe it was intended as a quiet rest stop for weary travellers, and nothing more.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPLandingPad_Content: Inspecting the plates on the ground, you find buried layers of scorch marks that could only have been made by spacecraft landing or taking off. Whoever lived here must have been capable of interstellar travel.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPArches_Content: Inspecting the structures closer, you detect a series of interlinked magnetic fields. You figure the arches were part of a transportation network built to quickly ship heavy materials up and down the mountains.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPFallenSatellite_Content: It's a scanner satellite from when the Lightyear Frontier was first discovered. Its mission long over, it must have fallen out of orbit and come to an end here.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPMonumentSign_Content: It's clear to you that this monument was built to sit down and gaze at something, but you have no clue what that something could be. It still makes for a pretty nice view of the fields below, at least.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPHarbourCrane_Content: Judging by the still-standing cranes, you're pretty sure this used to be a harbor. However, you can't find anything that looks like a ship in the vicinity.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPHarbourOutskirts_Content: Squinting at the horizon, you think you spot a landmass in the distance. Perhaps ships used to ferry goods between here and there.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPVaultSleepingChamber_Content: The chamber is filled with living quarters, extending deep into the underground. It looks like they tried to cram the population of a small city into one place. Doesn't look like anyone's home though.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPVaultComputerPedestal_Content: The computer is still on, but you have no idea how to operate it. You could start mashing random buttons to see if something happens, but you don't want to risk initiating an inexplicable self-destruct sequence.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPDam_Content: The dam in front of you appears fully intact and still functional after who-knows-how-long. Whatever material it was built with is clearly sturdier than anything made by human hands.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPMountainWall_Content: The doors are sealed shut, so no luck there. You can tell the exterior was made to keep solicitors out, but there's a surprising amount of artistic flair as well.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPVaultEntrance_Content: The energy coursing through the structure hums quietly. But other than that, it is silent as the grave.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPHallOfFame_Content: The walls are covered in ancient tablets. While time has taken its toll on them, it's clear most were originally broken by force. You try to read what little text remains, but it is written in a language you cannot comprehend.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPVaultComputerWindow_Content: These computer servers are likely filled with invaluable records of the ancient civilization before it came to an end. You make a note to send a tip to the Exoarchaeological Society in case they want to check this out.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPFactory_Content: Time has erased most of the great structure that once stood here, but you still find plenty of clues toward its former purpose. This was once a factory, capable of producing unfathomably huge machinery.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPStatue_Content: While you're no certified exoarchaeologist, the statue strikes you as perhaps the "least ancient" of all the ruins you've found so far. It may have been one of the last structures built before the civilization came to an end. Perhaps the sapling-like shape was meant to symbolize regrowth and renewal.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPBigSkeleton_Content: You do a bit of arithmetic and quickly conclude that a creature this size could not possibly support its own weight. Either the atmosphere and gravity of this planet were vastly different in the far past or someone did some extremely irresponsible gene splicing.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPBioLabTubes_Content: You inspect the tubes, clearly the remains of an experiment. It looks like this entire facility was dedicated to some kind of plant research.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPBioLabFlooding_Content: You look into the murky depths of the flooded facility. There's no telling what other experiments may have been conducted down there, and you're suddenly not too eager to find out.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPVaultHologram_Content: You recognise the hologram as a live updating map of the surface. It appears the vault's denizens diligently monitored the outside world.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPHarbourContainers_Content: You search through a few of the containers. It looks like they contained food supplies, but whatever wasn't already scavenged has long since turned to dust.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPBigBulbTree_Content: You try striking the bark but can't even make a scratch. This tree is made of sturdier stuff than most, probably explains why it's grown so big.
DT_InspectionPoints.IPBigTree_Content: You've seen a lot of weird stuff on this planet but you get the distinct feeling that a tree could not possibly grow this big through natural means. You'd bet your left boot on it.
