DT_OnDemandTutorials.FarmAnimals_Content:
<Mark1>Abandoned Eggs</> can be found in the wild. Use the <Mark1>Egg Incubator</> to hatch <Mark1>Abandoned Eggs</>. Hatching an egg will create a <Mark1>Farm Animal</> that will live on your farm. 

Different Lenses can be used in the Egg Incubator, each with a different effect on the hatched Farm Animal.

Farm Animals are different from wild Animals in a variety of ways. 

Farm Animals can be picked up and moved around. They will stay close to where they were hatched or wherever you place them. 

Farm Animals have needs that must be met in order for them to be <Mark1>Happy</>. Happy Farm Animals have a chance to drop resources and Droppings, which can be turned into Fertilizer. 
Farm Animals need space to move around, Fodder to eat, water to drink, and other Farm Animals to live with. Without these things, Farm Animals may become <Mark1>Unhappy</>. 
Farm Animals can be petted, which will increase their happiness once per day.
If a Farm Animal is Unhappy for too long, it will eventually run away from your farm and become a <Mark1>Runaway</>. A Runaway will move around in the same Region as where you kept them. If you don’t bring them back to your farm, they will disappear permanently after a few days.  

Unlike wild Animals, Farm Animals can’t be fed directly. They only eat Fodder placed in a <Mark1>Feed Trough</> that they can reach. Each animal has a specific Fodder they eat.
Farm Animals will only drink from <Mark1>Water Troughs</>, which need to be refilled periodically.

Use the <Mark1>Animal Dresser</> to craft and equip <Mark1>Clothing</> for Farm Animals. A Farm Animal must be placed on the Animal Dresser to equip <Mark1>Clothing</>.

To release a Farm Animal into the wild, pick it up with the Mech and bring it to an Animal Nest. A released Farm Animal will be gone permanently.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Watering_Content:
<Mark1>Seeds</> need to be watered every day in order to grow. Use your <Mark1>Watering Can</> or <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> to water <Mark1>Seeds</> in <Mark1>Mounds</>.

Rain will automatically water your crops. 

<Mark1>Sprouts</> - trees and large plants - only need water on the first day they’re planted.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Automators_Content:
Automators can enable some <Mark1>Crafting Constructs</> to repeatedly craft a recipe as long as the Automator has the required resources in its storage.

The Automator must be connected to the <Mark1>Crafting Construct</> by constructing <Mark1>Connector Pipes</> from the <Mark1>Output</> on the front of the Automator to an available <Mark1>Input</> on the back of the target <Mark1>Crafting Construct</>. Once connected, the <Mark1>Set Auto-Craft</> command becomes available in the target <Mark1>Crafting Construct's</> crafting menu and can be used to select a recipe to craft repeatedly. 

The Automator can be connected to multiple <Mark1>Crafting Constructs</> by combining <Mark1>Connector Pipes</> and <Mark1>Splitters</>, but each Automator has a max limit of <Mark1>Crafting Constructs</> it can automate.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.DamageAndRepairs_Content:
Both the Mech and the Exofarmer can take damage.

Each Part of the Mech can be damaged. If a Part reaches 0%, it will stop functioning. This has various effects depending on the Part. Any Mods installed on the Part will also stop functioning.
Use the Exofarmer’s <Mark1>Repair Drill</> to repair any damage to the Mech. The <Mark1>Repair Drill</> uses <Mark1>Repair Kits</>, which can be crafted in the <Mark1>Assembler</>.

The Exofarmer does not have part-specific damage. If the Exofarmer takes too much damage they will be knocked out, causing them to drop everything in their inventory and wake up back at home.
The Exofarmer can recover health by spending time inside the <Mark1>Home Area</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.FarmCenterHomeArea_Content:
Building a Farm Center will create a Home Area, which has many benefits. 

You can travel to a Farm Center once per day.

Dangers can’t enter the Home Area.

Remote storage access is available within the Home Area. Resources stored within a Home Area can be used in Crafting Constructs if they are within the same Home Area.

Decorations placed within a Home Area generate beneficial Coziness Effects when sleeping in a Bed.

The Home Area is indicated by a force field at its borders.

A limited number of Farm Centers can exist at one time. The Home Area size can be upgraded, and multiple Home Areas can be overlapped to create one larger Home Area.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Crafting_Content:
Crafting is essential to building your new life on the Lightyear Frontier.
Crafting is done inside Crafting Constructs, which can be built by opening {ToggleBuildMode} <Mark1>Build Mode</>.

Different Crafting Constructs are used to craft different things. 

Discovering new resources often unlocks new crafting recipes. Make sure to check back in on your Crafting Constructs whenever you discover a new resource.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Drones_Content:
Drones can be used to automatically water Mounds. 

When active, they will seek out the nearest Mound that has not been watered and is in the same <Mark1>Home Area</> as the Drone’s Station.

If a Drone runs out of <Mark1>Water</>, it will return to its Station.

Drones can work while you sleep.

Drones must be placed within a <Mark1>Home Area</> in order to function.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.FeedingWildAnimals_Content: Feeding wild Animals has a chance to drop resources and Droppings, which can be turned into Fertilizer.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Fertilizer_Content:
Fertilizer can be used to change the type of <Mark1>Mounds</>.

Use the <Mark1>Compost Box</> to turn compostable materials into Fertilizer.

Use the <Mark1>Fertilizer Spreader</> to apply Fertilizer to <Mark1>Mounds</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.ToolSelectionandchangingammunition_Conte:
Hold {OpenToolSelect} to open <Mark1>Tool Selection</>.
In <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, you can switch between different tools.
The Mech and Exofarmer have separate tools.

While in <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, hover on a tool and press {PreviousToolAmmo} and {NextToolAmmo} to change its ammunition.
Tools and ammunition can also be changed outside of <Mark1>Tool Selection</> by pressing {CycleEquippedTool} and {CycleEquippedAmmo}.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Deliveries_Content:
Making <Mark1>Deliveries</> is the main way to make progress.

Make <Mark1>Deliveries</> to complete <Mark1>Milestones</>, which unlock new Constructs, Mech Parts, Mods, and other useful things.

Each completed <Mark1>Delivery</> will increase your Mech's <Mark1>Power Limit</>, allowing you to equip more powerful Tools, Mech Parts, and Mod Chips on your Mech.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.PartCrafting_Content:
Mech Parts are the main pieces of the Mech and are necessary for it to work. 

Different Mech Parts have different Durability, Power Cost, and number of Mod Slots.
Durability determines how much damage a Mech Part can take before breaking. 
Power Cost determines how much a part counts toward the Mech's Power Limit.
The amount of Mod Slots determines how many Mods can be equipped on a Mech Part.

Craft Mech Parts in the Parts Crafting section of the Mech Depot.

Equipped Mech Parts count towards the Mech’s Power Limit.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.ModCrafting_Content:
Mods can be used to enhance the mech with various bonuses and new abilities.
Craft Mods in the Mods Crafting section of the Mech Depot.

Equipped Mods count towards the Mech’s Power Limit.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Harvesting_Content: Once a plant is fully grown, they can be harvested by hand or using the <Mark1>P.I.C.K.-Axe</>, <Mark1>Spike Saw</>, or <Mark1>Field Harvester</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Planting_Content:
Plants can be turned into <Mark1>Seeds</> using the <Mark1>Seed Extractor</>.

<Mark1>Seeds</> need to be planted in <Mark1>Mounds</> in order to grow. Use your <Mark1>Seed Bag</> or <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> to plant <Mark1>Seeds</> in <Mark1>Mounds</>. 

Plants will grow each day as long as they’re watered. Different plants take different amounts of time until they are fully grown.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Jetpack(Exofarmer)_Content:
Press and hold {Boost} while on the ground or press {Boost} while in mid-air to activate
Press or hold {Boost} to ascend
Press or hold {Sprint} to descend
Double press {Sprint} or land on the ground to deactivate

The <Mark1>Jetpack</> can be used by the Exofarmer to fly freely within Home Areas, or get some extra air time outside of it. 

Tools and Build Mode can be used while the Jetpack is active.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.RecyclingMode_Content:
Press {BuildModeRecycle} while in <Mark1>Build Mode</> to enter <Mark1>Recycling Mode</>. 
While in <Mark1>Recycling Mode</>, highlight any Construct or Decoration and press {BuildModeRecycle} to recycle it. Any resources that were used to build it will be returned.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.BuildModeandplacingbuildings_Content:
Press {ToggleBuildMode} while in the Mech or as the Exofarmer to open <Mark1>Build Mode</>. 

With a Construct or Decoration selected, press {Build} to place it. 

Press {ToggleBuildingSnap} to toggle whether the placement should snap to other Constructs and Decorations or not.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.RestoringRegions_Content:
Restore a Region by cleaning <Mark1>Noxious Weed</> and <Mark1>Noxious Slime</>. 

Restoring a Region causes resources to appear and Animals to return.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Energy_Content:
Some Constructs can become <Mark1>Energized</>, increasing their operating speed while the <Mark1>Energy</> demand is met.

This applies to any Construct with an <Mark1>Energy Cost</>. When such a Construct is built, its <Mark1>Energy Cost</> is subtracted from the <Mark1>Energy Meter</>, as seen in the top right of the HUD.
When the <Mark1>Energy Meter</> is in the negatives, the power grid is <Mark1>Overloaded</> and the Constructs operate at regular speed.

<Mark1>Generator Constructs</>  add <Mark1>Energy</> to the <Mark1>Energy Meter</>. <Mark1>Generator Constructs</> usually require <Mark1>Fuel</> to operate but will pause their fuel consumption until their combined output equals or exceeds the <Mark1>Energy</> demand.

Once the <Mark1>Energy Meter</> is at 0 or above, all Constructs with an <Mark1>Energy Cost</> become <Mark1>Energized</>. This has different effects for different types of Constructs.

<Mark1>Crafting Constructs</>: Recipes are crafted <Mark1>2.5x</> faster.
<Mark1>Water Pumping Constructs</>: Water flows <Mark1>2x</> faster.
<Mark1>Drone Stations</>: Drones move and act <Mark1>2x</> faster.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Storage_Content:
Storage Constructs are used to store resources.
Resources stored within a Home Area can be used in Crafting Constructs if they are within the same Home Area.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.IrrigationHose(Mech)_Content:
The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> waters plants and removes <Mark1>Noxious Slime</>.

Water for the <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> can be refilled by standing in shallow water or using the <Mark1>Vacuum Harvester</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.VacuumHarvester(Mech)_Content: The <Mark1>Resource Vacuum</> gathers water and loose resources.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.SeedShooter(Mech)_Content:
The <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> plants <Mark1>Seeds</> in <Mark1>Mounds</>.

With the <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> selected in <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, press {PreviousToolAmmo} and {NextToolAmmo} to change its ammunition. 
Ammunition can also be changed outside of <Mark1>Tool Selection</> by pressing {CycleEquippedAmmo}.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.SproutCannon(Mech)_Content:
The <Mark1>Sprout Cannon</> plants <Mark1>Sprouts</> - trees and large plants -  in the ground.

With the <Mark1>Sprout Cannon</> selected in <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, press {PreviousToolAmmo} and {NextToolAmmo} to change its ammunition.
Ammunition can also be changed outside of <Mark1>Tool Selection</> by pressing {CycleEquippedAmmo}.

<Mark1>Sprouts</> only need to be watered once, unlike <Mark1>Seeds</> which need to be watered every day.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Sleep_Content:
The Exofarmer can sleep by using a <Mark1>Bed</>.
Sleeping will cause the day to end and the next one to begin.
During this time, plants will grow, some resources may return in the world, and the weather may change. 
The Exofarmer will also gain health and available Coziness effects. 
If the Exofarmer is Knocked Out, they will wake up in the last Bed they slept in.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Neighbors_Content:
The Lightyear Frontier is home to a variety of people, some of whom will want to visit your planet.

A <Mark1>Neighbor</> can be assigned to a <Mark1>Neighbor Outpost</>. Once assigned, they will start regularly visiting your planet. 

Doing Missions for Neighbors and giving them items will increase their Friendship Level, which unlocks rewards, new dialogue, and additional Missions.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.MechModes_Content:
The Mech can be equipped with different <Mark1>Transform Modes</>. 

While inside the Mech:
Hold {Transform} to select Mech <Mark1>Transform Mode</>.

<Mark1>Pathmaker Mode</> creates paths on the ground.

<Mark1>Fertilizer Spreader</> applies Fertilizer to Mounds

<Mark1>Field Plow</> creates <Mark1>Mounds</> on the ground.

<Mark1>Lawnmower</> cuts grass.

<Mark1>Trailblazer Mode</> moves quickly.

<Mark1>Field Harvester</> harvests plants.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.ExitingandenteringtheMech_Content:
While in the Mech, hold {Disembark} to exit the Mech.
While outside the mech, look at the Mech and hold {Interact} to enter the Mech.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Fuel_Content:
While the Mech is <Mark1>Fueled Up</>, its tool speed is increased and Mod Chip effects and the Boost Lunge ability are enabled.

The mech spends <Mark1>Fuel</> by moving around and performing actions. 

Craft Oils in the <Mark1>Oil Presser</> to use as <Mark1>Fuel</> for the Mech. Oils drain at different rates. 

You can also get <Mark1>Fuel</> by picking up <Mark1>Refillite</> in the wild.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.Hoe(Exofarmer)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Create Mound
{UseToolSecondary} Remove Mound

Use the <Mark1>H.O.E.</> to create and remove <Mark1>Mounds</> on the ground. <Mark1>Mounds</> are used for planting <Mark1>Seeds</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.PaintGun(Exofarmer)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Fire Paint Gun

The <Mark1>Paint Gun</> can be used to paint Constructs and Decorations.

With the <Mark1>Paint Gun</> selected in <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, press {PreviousToolAmmo} and {NextToolAmmo} to change its ammunition. 
Ammunition can also be changed outside of <Mark1>Tool Selection</> by pressing {CycleEquippedAmmo}.

Use the <Mark1>Flower Press</> to create paint from <Mark1>Colorflowers</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.RepairDrill(Exofarmer)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Repair

Use the <Mark1>Repair Drill</> to repair any damage to the Mech. The <Mark1>Repair Drill</> uses <Mark1>Repair Kits</>, which can be crafted in the <Mark1>Assembler</>.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.SpikeSaw(Mech)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Spike Punch 
{UseToolSecondary} Saw Swipe

The <Mark1>Spike Saw</> is used to break things.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.P.I.C.K.-Axe(Exofarmer)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Strike 
{UseToolSecondary} Grab

The <Mark1>P.I.C.K.-Axe</> can break plants and minerals.

Use {UseToolSecondary} Grab to grab items.
Hold {UseToolSecondary} and press {UseToolPrimary} to Launch items.
Launch items at the Mech to add them to the Inventory.
DT_OnDemandTutorials.WateringCan(Exofarmer)_Content:
{UseToolPrimary} Water

Use the <Mark1>Watering Can</> to water <Mark1>Mounds</>.

DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Wildernest_PlanetBio: A heavily forested planet teeming with rare flora and the current target of the Pioneer's Guild. Most planets in the Frontier remain uncharted and Wildernest's deep jungles make it an even greater challenge.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Prometheus_PlanetBio: A research station that travels between planets in pursuit of higher knowledge. Filled with the sharpest minds in the Frontier, their greatest obstacle is agreeing on where to go next.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Hewstone_ColonyStatus: Lacking arable land, Hewstonians rely on trading for food from other planets. A recent trade embargo enforced by the Central Frontier Directorate has created a massive shortage, leaving them to deal with smugglers and independent traders for survival.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Verdemere_PlanetBio: Large, lush, and bountiful. Verdemere could be considered a paradise if not for the fact that its atmosphere is toxic to humans. Undeterred, some have chosen to implant cybernetic lungs in order to live a life of prosperity on the planet.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Hewstone_PlanetBio: Originally a mining outpost built in a precarious position. The cracked planet has an uninviting low-gravity terrain but the exposed abundance of rare crystals makes it all worth it.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Tempest_PlanetBio: Tempest Prime is an extremely dense planet with a rich metal crust. The home of the faithful of the Steel Pillar, it has a permanent cloud cover that frequently breaks out into geomagnetic storms, knocking out any electrical equipment in the vicinity.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Wildernest_ColonyStatus: The Guild has been sustaining its expedition on the many fruits available on the planet, which have turned out to lack sufficent nutrients. Now, suffering from various deficiencies, they are in dire need of a complementary source of food.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Homeworld_PlanetBio: The central authority of the Frontier, trying to coordinate the numerous colonies into a somewhat functional society.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Homeworld_ColonyStatus: The first colony of the Lightyear Frontier.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Cryolis_ColonyStatus: The harsh seas require appropriate thermal gear, but there are few sources of insulators as the planet has no native mammals and the permafrost prevents agriculture.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Cryolis_PlanetBio: The icy waters of Cryolis teem with aquatic life, keeping its seafaring denizens fed, as long as they dress for the weather.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Tempest_ColonyStatus: The population has long lived in underground tunnels to escape the storms. But as the ever deeper tunnels have grown unstable, the Pillarists have launched a project to construct habitable metal domes on the surface.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Prometheus_ColonyStatus: While able to indefinitely sustain its population of scientists, the station is often lacking sufficient materials for their research.
DT_PlanetDeliveryinfo.Verdemere_ColonyStatus: While prosperous, Verdemere lacks some of the base resources necessary to construct and maintain cybernetic implants. Unfortunately, most of their organic goods are toxic to offworlders, limiting their ability to trade.

DT_Quests.RadioTowerV2_Description: A Radio Tower will be useful for long-distance communications. Clear out the area and build the Radio Tower.
DT_Quests.TradingRoute_Description: A travelling merchant wants to do business on your planet. Build a Merchant Landing so they can land.
DT_Quests.GatherTools_Description: All the Mech's tools were scattered in the crash. Follow the orange smoke to find them.
DT_Quests.Automator_Description: Automators can auto-craft a recipe in connected crafting constructs, as long as they have the necessary items.
DT_Quests.Energy_Description: Boost your production by building Energy-generating constructs.
DT_Quests.Sleep_Description: Build a Homestead building and use it to go to sleep. This will start a new day.
DT_Quests.Coziness_Description: Build decorations to increase the Coziness of your home.
DT_Quests.FeedCritter_Description: Craft Fodder to feed animals. In return, they might find resources for you. Taking care of this world's animals is important!
DT_Quests.DianeDecor2_Description: Diane wants to decorate her Outpost some more.
DT_Quests.DianeDecor1_Description: Diane wants to decorate her Outpost.
DT_Quests.DianeHouse_Description: Diane wants to move to your planet. She has asked you to help her build a more permanent place to live.
DT_Quests.DianeEggScraps_Description: Diane wants to study the eggs on your planet. She has asked you to collect Egg Shards in the wild.
DT_Quests.DianeHappyAnimals_Description: Diane wants to test your animal handling skills. She has asked you to have multiple happy animals on your farm simultaneously.
DT_Quests.DianeBuildOutpost_Description: Diane wants to visit your planet, but she needs a place to stay. Build an Outpost so she can come visit.
DT_Quests.ReservoirDrone_Description: Distribute water to your Watering Drone Stations by connecting them to a Water Reservoir.
DT_Quests.Delivery_Description:
It’s time to start making Deliveries to neighboring colonies by using the Resource Delivery Cannon.
Making Deliveries completes Milestones, which unlocks various rewards.
DT_Quests.ModChips_Description: Learn to craft Mods for the Mech. Mods can be used to enhance the Mech with various bonuses and new abilities. Craft Mods in the Mods Crafting section of the Mech Depot.
DT_Quests.MechParts_Description: Learn to craft Parts for your Mech. Parts are the main pieces of the Mech and are necessary for it to work. Craft Parts in the Parts Crafting section of the Mech Depot.
DT_Quests.Fuel_Description: Learn to make Oil in the Oil Presser to use as Fuel. Fuel is vital to keep the Mech running.
DT_Quests.CraftBuff_Description: Learn to make your crafting more efficient by building structures around your Crafting Constructs.
DT_Quests.OpenVault_Description: Something activated when you restored nature. Find the strange structure and discover what lies within.
DT_Quests.Craft_Description: Start crafting Seeds in order to farm. Plants must be turned into Seeds in the Seed Extractor to be planted.
DT_Quests.BasicBase_Description: The ship is too damaged to function. Recycle it for parts to start building your new home.
DT_Quests.Seed_Description: Time to start your farm. Use the Exofarmer's Seed Bag or Mech's Seed Shooter to plant Seeds in Mounds.
DT_Quests.UlfBuildLab_Description: Ulf has asked you to use the Seed Splicer to create a Spliced Seed. Use a Spliced Seed to grow a Mutated crop and give it to Ulf.
DT_Quests.UlfDecor2_Description: Ulf wants to decorate his Outpost some more.
DT_Quests.UlfDecor1_Description: Ulf wants to decorate his Outpost.
DT_Quests.UlfBigTree_Description: Ulf wants to investigate the giant tree. He has asked you to go and collect samples for him.
DT_Quests.UlfHouse_Description: Ulf wants to move to your planet. He has asked you to help him build a more permanent place to live.
DT_Quests.UlfBuildOutpost_Description: Ulf wants to visit your planet, but he needs a place to stay. Build an Outpost so he can come visit.
DT_Quests.Upgrade_Description: Upgrade the Mech to improve it and unlock new abilities.
DT_Quests.Paint_Description: Use paints to color Constructs or the Mech.
DT_Quests.FindMech_Description: You survived the crash without any injuries, but the Mech is nowhere in sight. You'll need to find it before you can explore the planet any further.

DT_Requests.Wheat10_Description: A customer told me they're looking for a bundle of Wheat, you got some to spare?
DT_Requests.PlantOil10_Description: Client's got some engine parts that need oiling. You up for a job?
DT_Requests.Rabbage5_RewardText: Delivery confirmed. Transferring credits to you now.
DT_Requests.Rabbage5_Description: Eating Rabbages is the new health craze sweeping the sector. Work with me here and we can both make some money.
DT_Requests.Polyberry15_RewardText: Fascinating! Thank you for the data.
DT_Requests.ClearLens2_RewardText: Finally, now I can continue my work!
DT_Requests.PlantOil10_RewardText: Good work! Transferring the credits to you now.
DT_Requests.GloWood10_Description: Got a customer with a need for Glo Wood. Think you can get your hands on some?
DT_Requests.RedCrystal_Description: Got an order for some Red Crystals, send some my way and I'll make it worth your while.
DT_Requests.Chromaize5_Description: I don't get the Chromaize craze but if people are buying, I'm selling. Care to help me keep my stock up?
DT_Requests.Rabbage10_Description: I hear a restaurant on Homeworld wants to spice up their salad options. I think your Rabbages are just what they need!
DT_Requests.AluminiumElectronics2_RewardText: I knew I could count on you!
DT_Requests.AluminiumElectronics2_Description: I need some Aluminium Electronics for an urgent repair job. Can you get your hands on some?
DT_Requests.Caroot10_Description: I would like to make my favorite meal - root stew. The Caroots on your planet should produce a similar flavor profile. You wouldn't happen to have any, would you?
DT_Requests.CarootJuice3_Description: I'd like to run an experiment on distilled Caroot Oil, could you get me some?
DT_Requests.Polyberry15_Description: I'm curious about the Polyberries found on your planet. Could you plant some and send me their growth data?
DT_Requests.ElectricPlant5_Description: I'm curious about the Zappertwigs found on your planet. May I have a few for my experiments?
DT_Requests.GloFruit15_Description: I'm running an experiment on bioluminescense, would you happen to have any Glo Fruits you could send me?
DT_Requests.BlueColorflower8_Description: I'm running out of ink for my note-taking, but I could make some out of Blue Colorflowers. Do you have any to spare?
DT_Requests.Caroot30_Description: I'm trying to do an experiment on the tunneling properties of Caroot tubers, would you do me a favor and send 5 of them my way?
DT_Requests.Wheat150_Description: I've been sustaining myself on mushrooms, but I got a sudden craving for bread. Could you send me a shipment of Wheat so I can bake some?
DT_Requests.GloFruit5_Description: I've got a customer looking for Glo Fruit. You up for it?
DT_Requests.Polyberry10_Description: If you've got any Polyberries laying around, I'll take 'em off your hands.
DT_Requests.GloWood10_RewardText: Oh, they're gonna love this. Great work!
DT_Requests.RabbageSeeds5_Description: One of my customers wanted some new plants for their garden. You think you could get me some Rabbage Seeds?
DT_Requests.YellowGlass3_Description: One of my plant samples is proving to quite light sensitive, so I'd like to protect it with some Yellow Glass. Can you assist me in this?
DT_Requests.Caroot10_RewardText: Thank you! Now where did I put my saucepan..?
DT_Requests.Bloom5_RewardText: Thank you! With this, I promise to deliver never-before-seen results.
DT_Requests.CarootJuice3_RewardText: Thank you, now where did I put my liquid filter?
DT_Requests.BlueColorflower8_RewardText: Thank you, these will work beautifully!
DT_Requests.RabbageSeeds5_RewardText: Thanks, I knew I could depend on you.
DT_Requests.Rabbage10_RewardText: That should make for some fine cuisine, nice work!
DT_Requests.Bloom5_Description: The Lumbloom has fascinating bioluminescent properties. I would love a sample, if that's alright.
DT_Requests.ClearLens2_Description: The lenses on my microscope have worn down and it is now slowing down my work significantly. Could you help me?
DT_Requests.Caroot30_RewardText: These will do great, thank you.
DT_Requests.YellowGlass3_RewardText: This will do nicely, many thanks!
DT_Requests.GloFruit5_RewardText: Wow, they weren't kidding, these things really are bright.
DT_Requests.Wheat150_RewardText: You're doing me a huge favor, thank you.

DT_Tooltips.LargeObject_Tooltip: <Mark1>Large Objects</> can be hauled to a <Mark1>Sawmill</> or <Mark1>Mineral Crusher</> for increased yield.
DT_Tooltips.Roads_Tooltip: <Mark1>Roads</> grant a speed boost, reduce fuel costs, and make <Mark1>Trailers</> easier to pull.
DT_Tooltips.Trailers_Tooltip: <Mark1>Trailers</> are manufactured in the <Mark1>Fabricator</> and can be attached to the Mech to transport additional resources.
DT_Tooltips.AnimalRunaway_Tooltip:
A Farm Animal has run away due to being Unhappy. 
Return the Animal to your farm or it will disappear in a few days.
DT_Tooltips.IntroWall_Tooltip: Find your mech to explore further.
DT_Tooltips.CollectionMissions_Tooltip: Gifting special resources to Neighbors increases their Friendship Level and unlocks more missions.
DT_Tooltips.Jetpack_Tooltip: Hold {Button} while in the air to activate the Exofarmer's <Mark1>Jetpack</>.
DT_Tooltips.SecondTool_Tooltip: Hold {OpenToolSelect} to open <Mark1>Tool Selection</> or press {CycleEquippedTool} to switch Tools.
DT_Tooltips.SecondAmmo_Tooltip:
Hold {OpenToolSelect} to open <Mark1>Tool Selection</>.
While in <Mark1>Tool Selection</>, hover on a tool and press {PreviousToolAmmo} and {NextToolAmmo} to swap Ammo.
Or press {CycleEquippedTool}.
DT_Tooltips.Fuelled_Tooltip: Keep the Mech <Mark1>Fueled</> to increase tool speed and enable Mod Chips and Boost Lunge.
DT_Tooltips.FeedbackMenu_Tooltip: Press {MenuFeedback} to report bugs and give feedback.
DT_Tooltips.Trash_Tooltip: Remove <Mark1>Noxious Slime</> and <Mark1>Weeds</> to restore a region.
DT_Tooltips.SleepingBuilding_Tooltip: Sleep in Bed to start a new day.
DT_Tooltips.PlantSprout_Tooltip: Sprouts only need to be watered once.
DT_Tooltips.GetWater_Tooltip: Stand in water to refill the water tank, or use the <Mark1>Resource Vacuum</>.
DT_Tooltips.Hose_Tooltip: The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> waters plants and removes <Mark1>Noxious Slime</>.
DT_Tooltips.Vacuum_Tooltip: The <Mark1>Resource Vacuum</> gathers water and loose resources.
DT_Tooltips.SeedCannon_Tooltip: The <Mark1>Sprout Cannon</> plants Sprouts - trees and large plants - in the ground.
DT_Tooltips.Hauling_Tooltip:
The Mech can haul multiple <Mark1>Large Objects</>, limited by its <Mark1>Hauling Capacity</>.
{UseToolPrimary} Throw individual objects 
{UseToolSecondary} Drop all hauled objects
DT_Tooltips.DiscardAnimalHatch_Tooltip:
To release a Farm Animal into the wild, 
pick it up with the Mech and bring it to an Animal Nest.
A released Farm Animal will be gone permanently.
DT_Tooltips.DiscardAnimalNest_Tooltip:
To release a Farm Animal into the wild, 
pick it up with the Mech and bring it to an Animal Nest.
A released Farm Animal will be gone permanently.
DT_Tooltips.OnDemandTutorial_Tooltip: Tutorials can be found in the Database menu.
DT_Tooltips.OutOfFuel_Tooltip:
While the Mech is out of Fuel:
- Tool speed is decreased
- Mod Chips are disabled
- Boost Lunge is disabled
DT_Tooltips.GettingLate_Tooltip: {ToggleBuildMode} It's getting late. Open <Mark1>Build Mode</> to build a Bed.
DT_Tooltips.AllTools_Tooltip: {ToggleBuildMode} Open <Mark1>Build Mode</> to start building your farm.
DT_Tooltips.Jackhammer_Tooltip:
{UseToolPrimary} Spike Punch 
{UseToolSecondary} Saw Swipe 
The <Mark1>Spike Saw</> is used to break things.
DT_Tooltips.AcquirePick_Tooltip:
{UseToolPrimary} Strike 
{UseToolSecondary} Grab
The P.I.C.K. Axe can break trees, stones, and other materials.
DT_Tooltips.Repair_Tooltip: {UseToolPrimary} Use Repair Drill on damaged Mech Parts.
DT_Tooltips.GrabPick_Tooltip:
{UseToolSecondary} Grab
Hold {UseToolSecondary} and press {UseToolPrimary} to Launch.
Launch items at the Mech to add them to the Inventory.

DT_Upgrades.InventoryCapacity1_Description: Increase <Mark1>Inventory Capacity</>. The Mech can store more items before becoming Over-encumbered.
DT_Upgrades.Sprint1_Description: Increase <Mark1>Sprint speed</>, allowing the Mech to move faster.
DT_Upgrades.Thrusters1_Description: Increase <Mark1>Thruster Boost</>, allowing the Mech to jump higher and stay in the air for longer.
DT_Upgrades.WaterTankCapacity1_Description: Increase <Mark1>Water Tank Capacity</>. The Mech can store more water in its Water Tank.
DT_Upgrades.JackhammerDamage1_Description: Increase the Damage of the <Mark1>Spike Saw</>, allowing it to break materials in fewer hits.
DT_Upgrades.HosePressure1_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</>, allowing it to wash away strong <Mark1>Slime</>.
DT_Upgrades.PlatedMech1_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Mech Armor</>, allowing it to destroy stong <Mark1>Algae</>.WIP
DT_Upgrades.PlatedMech2_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Mech Armor</>, allowing it to destroy stong <Mark1>Algae</>.WIP
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterPower1_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Seed shooter</>, allowing it to shoot strong  <Mark1>Bulbs</>. WIP
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterPower2_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Seed shooter</>, allowing it to shoot strong  <Mark1>Bulbs</>. WIP
DT_Upgrades.JackhammerBreaking1_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Spike Saw</>, allowing it to break strong materials.
DT_Upgrades.VacuumStrength1_Description: Increase the Power of the <Mark1>Vacuum Harvester</>, allowing it to suck up strong <Mark1>Weeds</>.
DT_Upgrades.HoseRange1_Description: Increase the range of the <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</>. Liquids can be sprayed farther.
DT_Upgrades.VacuumRange1_Description: Increase the range of the <Mark1>Vacuum Harvester</>, allowing it to reach resources from farther away.
DT_Upgrades.VacuumWidth1_Description: Increase the width of the <Mark1>Vacuum Harvester</>, allowing it to gather resources in a wide area.
DT_Upgrades.HoseRange3_Description: The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> can spray Liquids <Mark2>even farther</>.
DT_Upgrades.HoseSplashSize1_Description: The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</>'s <Mark1>Hydro Splash</> splashes Liquids in a <Mark2>larger</> area.
DT_Upgrades.HoseSplashSize2_Description: The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</>'s <Mark1>Hydro Splash</> splashes Liquids in an <Mark2>even larger</> area.
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterLockOns1_Description: The <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> can Lock On to up to <Mark2>18</> <Mark1>Mounds</>.
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterLockOns2_Description: The <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> can Lock On to up to <Mark2>32</> <Mark1>Mounds</>.
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterLockOns3_Description: The <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> can Lock On to up to <Mark2>64</> <Mark1>Mounds</>.
DT_Upgrades.JackhammerBreaking3_Description: The <Mark1>Spike Saw</> can break <Mark2>even stronger</> materials.
DT_Upgrades.JackhammerDamage3_Description: The <Mark1>Spike Saw</> can break materials in <Mark2>even fewer</> hits.
DT_Upgrades.JackhammerDamage2_Description: The <Mark1>Spike Saw</> can break materials in <Mark2>fewer</> hits.
DT_Upgrades.VacuumWidth2_Description: The <Mark1>Vacuum Harvester</> can gather resources in a <Mark2>wider</> area.
DT_Upgrades.Thrusters2_Description: The Mech can jump <Mark2>even higher</> and stay in the air for <Mark2>even longer</>.
DT_Upgrades.InventoryCapacity3_Description: The Mech can store <Mark2>even more</> items before becoming Over-encumbered.
DT_Upgrades.WaterTankCapacity3_Description: The Mech can store <Mark2>even more</> water in its Water Tank.
DT_Upgrades.InventoryCapacity2_Description: The Mech can store <Mark2>more</> items before becoming Over-encumbered.
DT_Upgrades.WaterTankCapacity2_Description: The Mech can store <Mark2>more</> water in its Water Tank.
DT_Upgrades.Plough_Description:
While inside the Mech:
Hold {Transform} to select Mech <Mark1>Mode</>.

<Mark1>Field Plow Mode</> allows the Mech to freely create fields of Mounds on the ground.
DT_Upgrades.Lawnmower_Description:
While inside the Mech:
Hold {Transform} to select Mech <Mark1>Mode</>.

<Mark1>Lawnmower Mode</> allows the Mech to freely cut grass on the ground.
DT_Upgrades.Pathmaker_Description:
While inside the Mech:
Hold {Transform} to select Mech <Mark1>Mode</>.

<Mark1>Pathmaker Mode</> allows the Mech to freely create Paths on the ground.
DT_Upgrades.Car_Description:
While inside the Mech:
Hold {Transform} to select Mech <Mark1>Mode</>.

<Mark1>Trailblazer Mode</> allows the Mech to travel long distances in a short amount of time.
DT_Upgrades.HoseSecondary_Description:
While using <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</>:
Hold {UseToolSecondary} to charge <Mark1>Hydro Splash</>, press {UseToolPrimary} to fire.

The <Mark1>Irrigation Hose</> can fire big blobs of liquid that can splash on multiple <Mark1>Mounds</> at the same time.
DT_Upgrades.SeedShooterSecondary_Description:
While using <Mark1>Seed Shooter</>:
Hold {UseToolSecondary} to <Mark1>Lock On</>, press {UseToolPrimary} to fire.

The <Mark1>Seed Shooter</> can Lock On to and automatically hit up to 9 <Mark1>Mounds</>.

Piper_Barks.Rain1: Excellent, the rain will partially assist with the watering of your crops!
Piper_Barks.Headlights0: It's getting dark. Don't forget to turn on your headlights!
Piper_Barks.Rain0: Looks like it's raining today, you won't have to water your crops as much!
Piper_Barks.NoFertilizer0: Oh no! You ran out of fertilizer. Fortunately, I found more in the ship!
Piper_Barks.HighQuality0: This harvest is of great quality! Good job!

ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Gratitude4: "A good effort, stranger. More like this, and we'll get these domes up and running eventually."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Gratitude0: "A little goes a long way, you have my thanks."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Gratitude2: "A step closer to a new future for my daughter, and all of Verdemere."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Gratitude3: "All Pillarists are in your debt, friend. Your efforts have made all the difference."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Gratitude3: "Another batch for the dome project. Thank you, friend."
ST_VOText.CryolisColonyMilestoneGratitude4: "As thanks for putting wind in our sails."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Greeting4: "At first, I tried reaching out to Verdemere for help. My mom says I should stay away from them because they 'gave up their humanity' or whatever, but the lady I was talking to over the radio was just the sweetest. Turns out it was for nothing, though. All their food is, like, extremely poisonous to outsiders."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Gratitude3: "Between your goods and my hawking, we'll have all of Cryolis in fresh gear before the season's over, haha!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Intro0: "Big news! The scouting mission is back and they've found the perfect location! It's got food that the scouts assured us won't slowly poison everyone this time! Only hitch is, it's pretty far away from here. Sooo we're going to need to stock up a lot of supplies to be able to move the entire camp over there. Will you keep supporting us?"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Gratitude4: "Didn't think you'd come through, appreciate it."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Gratitude0: "Few are willing to even talk with us, I appreciate it."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Greeting3: "For the first time in ages, my repair queue is in the single digits. Everyone's implants are in top shape. We have only you to thank."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Greeting2: "Funny story, stranger. The other day I was talking to another trader and it turns out they thought I was some kind of robot, saying how impressed they were with my AI. It's just a steel helmet, all pillarists wear one."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Greeting3: "Fur and feathers are natural insulators, but the wax made from Honeybottles is not half bad either."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Greeting3: "Gave moving to Hewstone some thought, friend. They seem like proper tempered folk, and their crystal mines might not be too dissimilar from our tunnels. But I heard they were going through a famine, got enough mouths to feed as it is."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Greeting1: "Good day, good day! Got more insulators for the research project?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Greeting2: "Good day, good day! Let's get another crew back on their feet, shall we?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Intro0: "Good day, good day! This is Captain Greene of the FV Labrador. Let me lay it out for you real quick, my crew's good as stranded for the winter. Our gear is all patchy and we need proper insulators to make repairs, or we'll turn to popsicles next time we set out to sea, haha! We're happy to trade for anything that'll help keep us warm."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Greeting0: "Good to see you, buddy. Got more of your quality goods to spare?"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Gratitude3: "Good, I'll send you the Power Cores once the shipment arrives."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Greeting1: "Got any more microcrystal components? Our toxic atmosphere means I can't send you anything organic, but I've got some spare Power Cores for that mech of yours for the trouble."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Greeting2: "Gotta say, buddy, all this plate-spinning is doing me good. I'd be worried sick about my crew if I had nothing to do, but conspiring with you and the good folks from Prometheus Station is a fine way to keep busy."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepGratitude0: "Great stuff! I'll make sure this gets to the people in the lab ASAP."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Greeting2: "Had a nightmare last night, friend. Dreamt I had to pack my bags and move to Cryolis, with nothing but my helmet and my raincoat. Nevermind the freezing cold, I don't even like fish! Pillar protect me..."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepGratitude4: "Hahaha, yes! I mean, what a generous donation to science."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Greeting3: "Half my time is spent convincing other captains to accept the goods you send. A crew's pride is no small thing, and it doesn't sit right with them to receive help without paying. So we end up haggling the day away, haha!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Greeting4: "Here to deliver or just to stare?"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Greeting2: "Hewstone belongs to its people. The Directorate has no claim to our planet, no matter how much they desire our crystal mines."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Greeting4: "Hey there, buddy. Sunny skies and good wind, I trust?"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Intro0: "Hi there, solid steel to you! I was just off the radio with the dome project people. Looks like they've made a lot of progress on the foundations, in big part thanks to you, friend! I hope you'll keep on delivering us those bio-electrical plants, they seem to be doing a bang-up job for the construction effort."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Greeting3: "Hope's getting infectious, friend. My coworkers have begun daydreaming of how they'll furnish their new homes when the domes are completed. I'm trying not to get too ahead of myself, we can't strike until the steel is hot, after all."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Gratitude1: "How did you even grow all this?"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepCountdownGreeting4: "Huh? Sorry, I was on my lunch break. Yeah we're not doing research right now, check back later!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Gratitude4: "I appreciate all you've done for us."
ST_VOText.VerdemereColonyMilestoneGratitude3: "I can breathe easy, thanks to you!"
ST_VOText.HewstoneColonyMilestoneGratitude0: "I can feed my family thanks to you."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Gratitude1: "I could hug you, buddy! We'd be nowhere without you!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Greeting4: "I don't like to think about it, but one day I might be forced to move away from Tempest Prime. If we can't get these domes going, there won't be much of a choice. Pillar willing, it won't happen."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Greeting0: "I forgot to tell you the real kicker - the artifical insulators will be made from fish scales, hah! We've only got an infinite supply here on Cryolis. When it's done, we'll be able to see to ourselves in the long run, no chance for the Directorate to muck things up again!"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Gratitude2: "I knew I could rely on your contribution. Each delivery makes a difference."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepFullContributionGreeting2: "I knew grassroots funding was the way to go after they cut my budget. In your face, Department of Finance and Responsibility!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Greeting1: "I owe it to my spouse to give our daughter a better future. They left their family behind on Cryolis to live with me, getting the lung implant to breathe our air, but unable to ever return. We were happy together, but I wish they didn't have to choose between us."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepFullContributionGreeting0: "I promise you we will treasure your contribution and make the utmost use of it. Our last prototype made our test Mech spontaneously combust but I have several improvements in mind, so the next one will absolutely probably not do that!”
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Gratitude4: "I see you won't let fear get in the way of a good deal."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Gratitude4: "I was right crooked this morning but you've gone and put a smile on my face, buddy!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Greeting1: "I won't mince words, we’re right deep in it as it is. Mind sharing some of that bounty of yours to get my crew back on its feet?"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Intro0: "I'd rather die than give in to the Directorate's demands, but I can't ask my people to make that same sacrifice. Your deliveries are helping us keep the stalemate going, but we need to find a permanent solution."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Gratitude1: "I'd say this is more than plenty, but who knows how long we need to endure."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Greeting1: "I'll admit, it's tough to watch your own crew take off without you, even for a short outing. But right now I can do more good sitting on my arse and manning the radio. Someone's got to put Cryolis back on track, might as well be me."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Gratitude1: "I'll make good use of these, I promise."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Greeting4: "I'm one of the lucky ones, I don't have as many symptoms as most other expeditioners. So I signed up for Quartermaster duty to try to get us the food we need, and here we are!"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Greeting4: "I'm short on time, we'll have to make this quick."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepCountdownGreeting3: "I'm still sketching out ideas for my next project, but trust me when I say it will be my greatest invention yet!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Greeting2: "I'm, like, really starting to feel like myself again these days. Turns out being iron, magnesium, and whatever-other-mineral-deficient really puts a damper on your personality. Who would've thought?"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Greeting2: "If I could, I'd just head over and gather the necessary materials myself. But the same implants that let us live on Verdemere makes regular air unbreathable."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Greeting3: "It was my failed negotiations that saddled us with the trade embargo, but maybe I can still set things right."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Gratitude0: "It'll be tough, but we can manage thanks to you."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Greeting0: "It's good to hear from you. Do you have a new delivery on the way?"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Greeting2: "It's good to see you, got more components on the way?"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Gratitude0: "It's probably a bit late at this point but... Thank you."
ST_VOText.CryolisColonyMilestoneGratitude3: "Let us repay your kindness, take this!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Greeting4: "Let's make this quick, I promised Shelley I would play with her and I'm a man of my word."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Greeting0: "Like, I didn't think I'd be cut out for quartermastering, though I kinda like talking to people. But I've always loved pioneering. Being out in nature, finding places nobody's ever been, making totally new discoveries... The Guild is my everything. Welp, that got a little deep, it's just really easy to talk to you."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Gratitude1: "Little by little, we're making progress. The end to our struggles is in sight."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Gratitude0: "Looks good, this'll help a lot of people."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Gratitude4: "Looks like the tide's turning. At this rate we'll be back on the waves before long!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepCountdownGreeting1: "Love the enthusiasm, unfortunately we're not collecting any donations right now."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Greeting2: "Make it brief, I need to get back to the mines soon."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Greeting0: "Might have a tough time hearing you today, friend. The dome team is flattening the steel sheets before fixing them on the frame and it's making an awful racket."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Gratitude1: "More materials for the dome project, looks like there might be hope yet."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Greeting4: "Moving the camp is always an exciting time. Preparing rations, rolling up the tents, dismantling the equipment, it's all a big communal effort!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Gratitude0: "My pals on the dome project wanted me to say thank you for them, you're making a difference."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Greeting3: "My people are starving, and the Directorate is to blame."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Greeting3: "Naturally-occurring Octazene is, like, actually insane, it's only been made in labs before. It's a total game changer for space travel! Heck, if you're crazy enough you could probably use it to fly a ship all the way back to Earth."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Greeting0: "Not now Shelley, dad's busy... Sorry about that, my daughter wanted my attention."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Intro0: "Now I can't thank you enough, buddy. My crew's all geared up and ready to cast off! But I think I'll let them do the first run without me. Truth be told, we're not the only crew that's been holed up in port with ragged clothes. Will you help me supply them with insulators as well?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Gratitude2: "Now that's the stuff. This'll keep us warm!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Intro0: "Oh my gosh, thank you for picking up! The expedition is a total mess right now. We need nutrient-rich food, or we'll have to abandon our mission! We didn't realize it at first, but all the fruits in this jungle have like no iron at all. Before we knew it, everyone's turned anemic!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepGratitude2: "Oh yes, it's all coming together! We'll get this project off the ground in no time!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Gratitude2: "On behalf of the Pioneer's Guild, you rock!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Greeting4: "One upside of staying ashore this long is that I get to see the grandkids every day. Normally, I'd barely see them until they were ready to join us on a voyage. They're some cute little sleeveens, I tell you!"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Greeting2: "Our hydroponic facility is making progress, but still a ways away from self-sufficiency."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS1Greeting3: "Our mechanical lungs require a lot of maintenance, or we won't be able to breathe Verdemere's toxic air. Hope you've got more components."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Greeting2: "Our mission here on Wildernest might be our most important ever. Our scans told us there are natural Octazene gas deposits somewhere in this jungle. With that, we could make ship fuel that's like a million times more potent, we'd be able to zoom from planet to planet at record pace!"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Greeting0: "Our most reliable smuggler. Do you have a new delivery for us?"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Greeting3: "Our population is really reaching its limits, stranger. All attempts to expand the tunnels have only lead to new cave-ins. The bedrock has grown too unstable."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Greeting1: "People think we're just adventuring for fun, but they couldn't be more wrong! The Pioneer's Guild is behind most of the major discoveries in the Frontier. There wouldn't be any crystal mines on Hewstone if we didn't find them first, and nobody would be fishing on Cryolis if we hadn't surveyed the oceans! But now it's like everyone forgot!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Gratitude2: "Piece by piece, the domes are nearing completion. We're on the home stretch, friend."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepOngoingGreeting3: "Prometheus Station is where all the cutting edge research in the Frontier is done. The stuffy old scholars in the Directorate University are too cowardly to pursue even half the fields of study we do here!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepDepletedEvents0: "Prometheus Station is where all the cutting edge research in the Frontier is done. Want proof? Check your mailbox for all the inventions we've made, all for free!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepFullContributionGreeting3: "Rest assured that your donation is in responsible hands. In the last quarter, we've pushed down our failure rates by a stunning 7%. We're now down to only 74% overall!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Greeting0: "Right, I should explain what I've been working on. I owe you that much. I'm trying to create a new type of artificial lung, one that works both on Verdemere and in regular atmospheres. It would let my people roam freely again, and we wouldn't need to rely on the goodwill of others to live."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Gratitude3: "Sending over the Power Cores, it's the least I can do."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Greeting3: "So get this, we analyzed some soil samples the scouts brought back from the new camp location, and we found traces of Octazene! Once we set up shop there, finding the deposits should be a breeze!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Greeting1: "Solid steel, friend. It's good to see you."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Greeting2: "Solid steel, friend. The ion storms have been especially fierce today, hope our connection won't be cut short. May the Pillar protect us."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Greeting4: "Solid steel, friend. What have you got for the Steel Pillar today?
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Greeting0: "Solid steel, stranger. Do you have materials for the Steel Pillar?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Greeting2: "Soon as we get our gear repaired, it's back out to sea for us. We've got plenty of fishing spots to trawl before the season's over."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Greeting0: "State your business and keep it short, or the Central Frontier Directorate might trace our connection."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Gratitude3: "Thank you once again. You've come through for us time after time."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Greeting0: "Thanks for reaching out, buddy. It's slow going finding a new trading partner these days. Most of them are going into the crystal trade now that Hewstone's blocked off. Won't do us much good though, we just want warm jackets for the wind and rain."
ST_VOText.TempestColonyMilestoneGratitude3: "Thanks to you, we may yet endure."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Intro0: "Thanks to you, we've got enough components to see to everyone's immediate needs, but we'll need to stock up more for a rainy day. Most of the Frontier likes to pretend we don't exist, but there's always a few willing to look past appearances. It's how I met my spouse, actually."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Greeting1: "Thanks to your provisions, we've been able to work full shifts in the mines again. It's looking more like the Hewstone I remember."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Gratitude1: "That's a right generous delivery, thanks buddy!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Gratitude0: "That's it! That's exactly what we need!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepGratitude1: "That's so genererous of you! I'd ask you for even more but the Department of Ethics and Morals is already on my case."
ST_VOText.HewstoneColonyMilestoneGratitude4: "The Foreman was right to trust you."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Gratitude4: "The Frontier could use more people like you."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Gratitude1: "The Pillar rises taller with your effort, friend."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Greeting1: "The dome project has been stalling for a while, and we need all the help we can get, else we might not be able to stay on this planet much longer."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Greeting3: "The embargo shut us off from our trading partners on Cryolis. The Directorate strong-armed them into submission. Their fish used to be our primary food source."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepOngoingGreeting4: "The process to getting material funding here on the station is a bureaucratic nightmare. Trust me when I say that we need all the donations we can get. So how about it, want to stick it to the man and support the true cutting-edge of science?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Gratitude2: "The research is chugging along, keep it coming, buddy!"
ST_VOText.CryolisColonyMilestoneGratitude1: "The whole crew came together for this!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Greeting0: "There's honestly not a lot of support for the Guild nowadays, everyone's mostly focused on their own planet. We haven't even explored a fraction of the Frontier, who knows what's out there? So, like, thanks for believing in us."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Intro0: "This is Hewstone Outpost, Foreman Lilith speaking. If you're not just another smuggler looking to scam us, we're in dire need of whatever food supplies you're willing to trade."
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepCountdownGreeting2: "This is the great Professor Antimony's auto-response hologram. She's not available for research at the moment but asks that you return later with, quote: 'moneybags at the ready'."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS1Gratitude3: "This should keep us toasty as a duck in a down coat, haha!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Gratitude1: "This should see another crew back on the waves before long."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Gratitude1: "This should stave off the hunger a little longer."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Gratitude3: "This should stave off the hunger for a while."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Gratitude0: "This will help our hydroponics research. Only by growing our own food can Hewstone become truly independent."
ST_VOText.VerdemereColonyMilestoneGratitude4: "We can only offer this in return."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Intro0: "We did it! Everyone's starting to get better! I- I thought it was over for a while there!.. Sorry, I nearly lost it there. *ahem* I have more quartermasterly duties to carry out. I gotta ask you for even more deliveries, we're trying to send a scouting mission to find a new base camp location with access to actually nutritious food this time."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Greeting4: "We got the schematics for the hydroponics facility from Prometheus Station. They stand by the principle that all knowledge should be free, even in the face of the Directorate's tyranny."
ST_VOText.VerdemereColonyMilestoneGratitude0: "We have plenty of blueprints, take them."
ST_VOText.HewstoneColonyMilestoneGratitude2: "We scraped together and got you this."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Gratitude2: "We should be able to make storm-resistant electronics out of these. Thank you, stranger!"
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Greeting0: "We used to get all components we needed from Hewstone, their Foreman is a decent sort. But the trade embargo has shut that door."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS1Greeting3: "We were making good progress on our mission for the first couple months, when people suddenly started feeling weak and tired all the time. At first we thought it was some kind of disease, but it turned out to be iron deficiency."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Greeting4: "We wouldn't be in this mess if the Directorate didn't decide to upend all trade in the Frontier. They seem to think they can strongarm us all into rejoining them and be one big happy family, but that ship has long since sailed."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Gratitude3: "We'll be able to make a few rations out of these."
ST_VOText.CryolisColonyMilestoneGratitude2: "We'll make it through the winter thanks to you!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Greeting1: "We're called a guild but we don't get all that many new recruits anymore. I guess we're more like a tribe these days. I'm a sixth-generation Guild member, my great-great-great-grandma was part of the original settling in the Frontier!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepCountdownGreeting0: "We're kind of in between projects right now, but check back later once we've got something going!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Gratitude2: "We're making a difference, you and I. Don't you forget it!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepFullContributionGreeting4: "We're making good progress on the project. I just need to shake down a few more suckers for donations to bring it home!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Gratitude4: "We're nearing a breakthrough. I can't wait to stick it to the Directorate!"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Greeting3: "We've got plenty more Power Cores to trade, got a bunch of them laying around ever since our ancestors scrapped their spaceships to build seacrafts."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Intro0: "We've only gone and done it, buddy! The whole town's blocked with activity, all crews are up and running. But listen to this, I got in contact with a lovely lass from Prometheus Station who's begun researching artificial insulators. But we'll be needing lots of material to study to develop it. Can I trust you to help us see this through?"
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Gratitude3: "What a haul! You're a right saint."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Gratitude2: "What's that? Just tell them yourself... Fine. Shelley says thank you for the delivery."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS1Greeting1: "What's your business? If you're not here to deliver food, save it."
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS3Greeting1: "While I'm really looking forward to resuming the expedition once we move camp, I think I'm also going to miss... This, y'know? Talking to you helped get me through some pretty dark times. I think I'll keep doing some quartermastering even when we're not facing a nutrition crisis."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS2Greeting1: "While we can't trade any organics due to their toxicity to unmodified beings, we're always ready to send mechanical prostheses to those who need them. Unfortunately, some people are too superstitious to accept."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Gratitude2: "With this, more stomachs can be filled. You have my gratitude."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Intro0: "With your latest shipment, we have enough components to last a generation. I think it's safe to say you've saved us from disaster. However, I've got a more permanent solution in mind, if you're still willing to help. It's going to require many more components, are you in?"
ST_VOText.WildernestColonyMilestoneGratitude1: "Without you, the expedition would be over!"
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS2Greeting2: "Y'know, before the Guild came here to Wildernest, I heard we were actually considering Tempest Prime as our next target. Buuut it was deemed way too dangerous what with the constant storms and all. No clue how the Pillarists manage to live there."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Greeting1: "You have my attention. Here about another delivery, I trust?"
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS2Gratitude4: "You have our thanks. Hewstone won't forget your contribution."
ST_VOText.TempestColonyMilestoneGratitude0: "You have the gratitude of the Steel Pillar!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS1Gratitude3: "You have the gratitude of the Steel Pillar. And, well, me too!"
ST_VOText.TempestRepS2Gratitude4: "You really did us a solid, friend. Pardon the pun."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Gratitude4: "You should see the grin on my face, friend. I can't wait to send this over to the dome team."
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS3Gratitude3: "You're a force of good in this world, I mean it."
ST_VOText.TempestRepS3Greeting4: "You're always a welcome sight, friend. The Steel Pillar is strengthened by your presence."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS2Gratitude0: "You're right reliable, buddy. I can always count on you to deliver, haha!"
ST_VOText.PrometheusRepGratitude3: "You're so kind, I could shake your hand! But only if we're in a fully sterile environment while wearing N95 masks and nitrile gloves."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Intro0: "You've bought us time to stave off a famine, and I intend to use it to overcome our reliance on trading for food. All future deliveries will now go towards establishing a hydroponic facility to grow our own food. I trust you'll help us on this journey as well."
ST_VOText.TempestColonyMilestoneGratitude1: "You've taught us all a lesson in tempering."
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS3Gratitude4: "Your contribution far outweighs the value of a few Power Cores. We're in your debt."
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS3Gratitude0: "buddy, you're welcome to join my crew anytime. You're just about family now!"
ST_VOText.RancherM6End1Line0: *Sigh*... No, you're right. It was a great service and I'm thankful for your kindness.
ST_VOText.RancherM3End0Line1: ... I guess what's done is done, thank you for the help.
ST_VOText.DamFound0Line2: ... Maybe it's just a huge art installation.
ST_VOText.ToolFound0Line4: ...You have no idea what I'm talking about, do you?
ST_VOText.RegionRestored2Line4: 3rd generation is, well, it took them two complete redesigns to make satellites that wouldn't break on the journey here.
ST_VOText.CraftingResource1Line0: A little bit of this, a little bit of that!
ST_VOText.GrasslandsVaultTunnel0Line0: A statement, or just a child's doodles?
ST_VOText.ToolFound3Line3: A testament to human tenacity, don't you think?
ST_VOText.TornadoAlert1Line0: A tornado is headed straight for our farm!
ST_VOText.BotanistM2Intro1Line1: A-and do not worry if the mutations are only aesthetic. The scientific method tells us that failure is-is also a result, just keep at it!
ST_VOText.BotanistDialogueReturn0Line0: A-anything else I can help you with?
ST_VOText.BotanistM6Intro1Line2: Again and again, why can't I do anything right?
ST_VOText.RestorationAction3Line0: Ah, peace of mind seeing that taken care of.
ST_VOText.BotanistGreeting2Line0: Ah, s-so nice to see you!
ST_VOText.BotanistM3Intro0Line0: Ah, yes. I-I have been studying that crop you gave me.
ST_VOText.RancherGC14Line0: All good things have to come to an end. The mystery of the eggs was a roadblock we simply could not pass, no matter what we tried.
ST_VOText.RancherM6Intro2Line1: All the different eggs from all over the planet have been in contact with a single specimen, the DNA is all a match!
ST_VOText.RancherM4End1Line0: All the eggs come from the same place!
ST_VOText.PRBotanistM1Mid2Line1: Alright, how do we get started?
ST_VOText.FindRTSpot0Line0: Alright, looks like this spot is our best bet to catch that signal from before.
ST_VOText.RancherBC1Line0: Always be on the lookout for stray eggs! They can turn up in the most unexpected places.
ST_VOText.MRSuggestionPlant0Line1: Always good to have more crops in the harvest cycle.
ST_VOText.VaultDoorOpening0Line0: Amazing, to think this was under my nose the whole time.
ST_VOText.PRRancherM1Mid0Line1: An omelette would be good right now actually...
ST_VOText.RuinFound2Line4: And I find most rocks fascinating!
ST_VOText.GrasslandsVaultFirstComputerPuzzle0Line3: And I think I just lost some decades’ worth of weather readings.
ST_VOText.SproutPlanted3Line0: And now we wait for the sprout to grow.
ST_VOText.MRRainyWeather1Line1: And that leaves a heap of time for other fun activities!
ST_VOText.BotanistM3End0Line2: And to then be able to come back home to this comfortable outpost is quite a privilege.
ST_VOText.GrasslandsVaultSleepingChamber0Line3: And who knows if there are even more spaces like this down here?
ST_VOText.RancherGC3Line0: Animals in the wild are strangely picky with their food. They seem to pick one flavor and stick with it, even if they're fully capable of eating a variety of things.
ST_VOText.RuinFound1Line0: Another ruin site! I wonder what it has in store for us.
ST_VOText.RuinFound3Line0: Another ruin... How can something so old and broken look so beautiful?
ST_VOText.MRRainyWeather1Line0: Any crops we have won't need watering since it's raining today.
ST_VOText.PRBotanistM6Intro0Line0: Any news on the megaflora experiment?
ST_VOText.BotanistM4Intro0Line4: Any risk of a resource shortage in the Frontier would be but a memory.
ST_VOText.MerchantDialogueReturn0Line0: Anything else I can do for you?
ST_VOText.GrasslandsVaultFirstComputerPuzzle0Line4: Anyway, I've started to get basic control of the system now.
ST_VOText.BotanistM2Intro0Line1: As I mentioned before, mutations are somewhat unpredictable. They do not always occur, th-therefore we need quite large sample sizes to increase the odds of a succesful harvest.
ST_VOText.BotanistWC3Line0: B-be careful where you step today, the fog can be quite treacherous sometimes.
ST_VOText.BotanistM4End0Line3: B-but, the tree's massive xylem level indicates this would have been done a very long time ago...
ST_VOText.BotanistGC7Line0: Back on my planet, I-I was once able to combine the leavening and fermenting properties of two entirely different species of fungi.
ST_VOText.BotanistM4End3Line1: Being out here by myself for so long, I had almost forgotten what it was like to have a confidant.
ST_VOText.GrasslandsVaultSecondComputerPuzzle0Line: Besides, I just got access to the computer's activity log!
ST_VOText.BotanistWC5Line1: Better make the most of it!
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Intro0: Big colony story finale goes here WIP
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS4Intro0: Big colony story finale goes here WIP
ST_VOText.TempestRepS4Intro0: Big colony story finale goes here WIP
ST_VOText.VerdemereRepS4Intro0: Big colony story finale goes here WIP
ST_VOText.WildernestRepS4Intro0: Big colony story finale goes here WIP
ST_VOText.RuinFound3Line5: Big difference that made, they cancelled the Farmech Project all the same.
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Gratitude0: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Gratitude1: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Gratitude2: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Gratitude3: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.CryolisRepS4Gratitude4: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS4Gratitude0: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS4Gratitude1: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS4Gratitude2: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
ST_VOText.HewstoneRepS4Gratitude3: Big gratitude though we're thriving WIP
